summary: The Docker-based reproduction demonstrates that call_user_func_array executes arbitrary JavaScript code through eval() when processing the cb[1] parameter. The crafted parameter successfully executes and outputs the proof string.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should execute attacker-controlled JavaScript code through eval() in call_user_func_array and emit a proof string.
actual_observed_result: The reproduction executed successfully. The eval() path was triggered through crafted cb parameter and code execution was demonstrated through proof string output [EVOHUNT-PROOF-call_user_func_array].
verification_method: The PoC calls call_user_func_array with a crafted array parameter where cb[1] contains injected code. The eval() at line 34 executes the injected code. The output confirms successful exploitation.
evidence_produced_by_run: Docker container built and executed successfully
eval() at line 34 executed with injected cb[1] parameter
Code execution demonstrated through proof string output

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh mounts the target repository at /target-repo for the container to access.
